code:
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards/multi-purchase failures overriding positives
    - Fundamental usability failures despite concept appeal
    - Explicit "avoid purchase" warnings with defect evidence
    - Premature failure (≤6 months) with functional consequences
    - Negative updates erasing previous satisfaction

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality requires excessive workarounds
    - Partial functionality with persistent frustration
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in critical aspects
    - "Regret purchase" statements with justification
    - Comparative inferiority without safety hazards

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with contextual limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with notable compromises
    - Target-specific adequacy without endorsement

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core utility
    - Requires preprocessing/break-in for full function
    - Explicit star deduction phrases ("only 4 because...")
    - Minimal functional praise without enthusiastic modifiers
    - Significant improvement without exceptional performance
    - Aesthetic praise without corresponding functional excellence
    - Practical benefits meeting needs without enthusiastic endorsement

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise enthusiastic praise with specific functional benefits
    - 3+ functional aspects praised (durability/design/utility)
    - Solves persistent problems exceeding expectations
    - Non-functional criticism WITH enthusiastic core praise
    - Close brand comparisons with daily-driver status
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor imperfections
    - Decorative items fulfilling aesthetic purpose perfectly

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - "Works as intended" →5 unless functional criticism exists
    - "Works well"/"They work" →4 vs "Works perfectly!" →5
    - Praise + functional flaw →4 / + non-functional →5
    - Multi-purchase failures →1 regardless of initial experience
    - Aesthetic praise + basic function →4 (unless decorative)
    - Core function achieved post-setup →5 if flawless execution
    - Brief effectiveness claims →5 only with concrete validation
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "Love the aqua design! Foldable and works for light storage"
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise + basic function →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Matches Lodge's heat retention! Daily driver despite weight"
<Reasoning>Brand parity + daily use →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "These napkins finally absorb! Huge improvement over old ones"
<Reasoning>Significant improvement without enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Solved our drawer chaos! Flimsy feel irrelevant to function"
<Reasoning>Core functionality excellence →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for seasonal decor. Wouldn't trust for heavy tools"
<Reasoning>Contextual limitation →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Constant recalibrations needed. Exhausting to use"
<Reasoning>Excessive workarounds →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "Non-stick perfection! Handle warmth irrelevant"
<Reasoning>Functional excellence with trivial issue →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Does job adequately. No complaints after 2 months"
<Reasoning>Minimal functional praise →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 10:
Review: "Stopped sealing after 5 months. Great until failure"
<Reasoning>Premature functional failure →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
artifacts_json:

  1 star: OK

  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

  gold 5 -> 4: 14
  gold 4 -> 5: 11
  gold 4 -> 3: 6
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 2 -> 1: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "This was pretty straightforward to put together, feels very sturdy, leaves lots of room underneath for storage (plus a little space I left open because the dog seems to be happy about being able to curl up under there and use it as a hiding place), a..."
     Gold: 2 | Predicted: 1 | Workers: [1, 3, 2] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "Made in the USA.  It's the only candles I'll buy since I've just read too much about what can be in foreign made candles.  Things like lead. (ugh)

Normally I buy Yankee candles but I found this Village Candle to be just as nice if not nicer.  The sc..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  4. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 2, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  5. Review: "I really like these potholders. They look great and do a good job of keeping hot pots and pans and other dishes out of the oven off the table and counters. They had a bit of a plastic smell when they arrived but after I washed them (they held up fine..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "There is only one problem for the experienced cook when it comes to this set - you're likely to already have one or more of these tools and possibly Oxo Good Grips at that! That's why I think this set is best suited for someone setting up a kitchen f..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "The towels are definitely cute and well made. Very attractive. The only reason Im not giving them five stars is because they aren't very absorbent. Out of the package they aren't really absorbent at all. Throw them in the wash with some fabric soften..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "Fit perfect into my CHV1210."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [3, 5, 5] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "I was actually looking for smaller pie cover; really only wanting something that would cover and keep my pumpkin pie from drying out when it was in the fridge.

For that purpose this Pie holder is overkill. It's LARGE. It's tall enough for meringues ..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "Grunds bathmat is 100% certified organic cotton  with the main section being long straight cotton fibers and it has three stripes of loops on each end. This fiber is not treated with bleach, formaldehyde,  or any other harmful toxic substances. This ..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 4, 3] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 38 errors, 18 borderline out of 80 examples."}
